metadata.description: Challenges validating performance optimizations introduced in the comprehensive audit: FormatRegistry lazy initialization, StyleSheets cache effectiveness, DocumentCache hit rates, Semaphore-controlled concurrent parsing, non-blocking I/O verification, and parse time budgets per format

challenges.name: FormatRegistry Lazy Initialization Verification
challenges.description: Verify that FormatRegistry.formats uses lazy { createFormats() } and does not initialize until first access. Check that isFormatsInitialized returns false before any format lookup, true after the first lookup. Measure the time from application startup to first format access and verify that no format parsing objects are created during static initialization.

outputs.name: is_lazy_initialized
outputs.description: Whether FormatRegistry.formats uses lazy {} delegate

outputs.name: initialized_before_access
outputs.description: Whether isFormatsInitialized was true before first access

outputs.name: initialized_after_access
outputs.description: Whether isFormatsInitialized was true after first access

outputs.name: lazy_init_time_ms
outputs.description: Time to initialize formats on first access

assertions.message: FormatRegistry.formats must use lazy {} delegate
isFormatsInitialized must be false before first format access
isFormatsInitialized must be true after first format access
Lazy format initialization must complete within 500ms

challenges.name: StyleSheets Cache Effectiveness
challenges.description: Verify that StyleSheets.styleSheetCache returns cached CSS on repeated calls with the same theme parameters. Measure first-call vs cached-call latency for both light and dark themes. Verify that clearCache() invalidates all entries and that subsequent calls regenerate CSS. Target: at least 50x speedup on cached calls.

outputs.name: first_call_avg_ms
outputs.description: Average time for first CSS generation call per theme

outputs.name: cached_call_avg_ms
outputs.description: Average time for cached CSS retrieval per theme

outputs.name: cache_speedup_factor
outputs.description: Speedup ratio of cached vs first call

outputs.name: clear_cache_works
outputs.description: Whether clearCache() properly invalidates all entries

outputs.name: css_content_identical
outputs.description: Whether cached CSS is byte-identical to first generation

assertions.message: StyleSheets cache must provide at least 50x speedup on cached calls
clearCache() must invalidate all cached stylesheets
Cached CSS must be byte-identical to originally generated CSS

challenges.name: DocumentCache Hit Rate Under Realistic Workload
challenges.description: Simulate a realistic editing workload against DocumentCache: parse 20 unique documents, then re-parse them in a Zipf-distributed access pattern (some documents accessed far more often than others) for 1000 total operations. Verify hit rate exceeds 80%, that LRU eviction works correctly when cache is full, and that hit/miss counters are accurate.

outputs.name: cache_hit_rate
outputs.description: Cache hit rate as a percentage (0-100)

outputs.name: total_hits
outputs.description: Total cache hits

outputs.name: total_misses
outputs.description: Total cache misses

outputs.name: lru_evictions
outputs.description: Number of LRU evictions that occurred

outputs.name: counter_consistent
outputs.description: Whether hits + misses equals total operations

assertions.message: DocumentCache hit rate must exceed 80% under Zipf-distributed workload
Cache hit + miss counters must equal total operations
At least one LRU eviction must occur to verify eviction logic

challenges.name: Semaphore-Controlled Concurrent Parsing (parseWithCacheConcurrent)
challenges.description: Verify that parseWithCacheConcurrent uses a Semaphore to limit concurrent parse operations and prevent resource exhaustion. Launch 50 concurrent parse requests and verify that at most N (the Semaphore permit count) are executing simultaneously. Measure throughput and verify no OOM or thread pool exhaustion occurs. Verify that requests beyond the Semaphore limit are queued, not rejected.

outputs.name: max_concurrent_observed
outputs.description: Maximum number of parse operations running simultaneously

outputs.name: all_completed
outputs.description: Whether all 50 parse requests completed successfully

outputs.name: oom_occurred
outputs.description: Whether an OutOfMemoryError occurred

outputs.name: queued_requests
outputs.description: Number of requests that waited for a Semaphore permit

outputs.name: total_throughput_docs_per_sec
outputs.description: Documents parsed per second

assertions.message: Maximum concurrent parses must not exceed Semaphore permit count
All concurrent parse requests must complete successfully
No OutOfMemoryError allowed under Semaphore-controlled parsing
At least one request must be queued to verify Semaphore backpressure

challenges.name: Non-Blocking I/O Verification for Protocol Services
challenges.description: Verify that file read/write operations in all 8 protocol services use non-blocking I/O (withContext(Dispatchers.IO) or equivalent) and do not block the main/calling coroutine dispatcher. Launch file operations on the Default dispatcher and verify that the dispatcher thread pool is not starved by measuring concurrent non-I/O task latency during file operations.

outputs.name: non_io_task_latency_ms
outputs.description: Average latency of non-I/O tasks running concurrently with file operations

outputs.name: dispatcher_starved
outputs.description: Whether the Default dispatcher was starved by I/O operations

outputs.name: services_using_io_dispatcher
outputs.description: Map of service to whether it uses Dispatchers.IO for file operations

outputs.name: services_tested
outputs.description: Number of protocol services tested

assertions.message: Default dispatcher must not be starved by file I/O operations
Non-I/O task latency must stay under 100ms during concurrent file operations
All 8 protocol services must be tested for non-blocking I/O
All services must use Dispatchers.IO for file operations

challenges.name: Parse Time Budgets Per Format
challenges.description: Verify that each of the 17 format parsers meets its parse time budget for standard-size documents. Budgets: Markdown 200ms (10KB), CSV 300ms (5000 rows), LaTeX 250ms (10KB), TodoTxt 100ms (500 items), OrgMode 200ms (10KB), WikiText 200ms (10KB), AsciiDoc 250ms (10KB), RST 200ms (10KB), Textile 150ms (5KB), Creole 150ms (5KB), BibTeX 200ms (5KB), JSON 100ms (50KB), XML 150ms (50KB), YAML 150ms (10KB), Properties 50ms (1000 lines), INI 50ms (500 sections), TOML 150ms (10KB). Run each parser 10 times and report p50 and p99.

outputs.name: format_results
outputs.description: Map of format ID to p50/p99 parse times and pass/fail status

outputs.name: formats_within_budget
outputs.description: Number of formats within their parse time budget at p99

outputs.name: formats_over_budget
outputs.description: Formats that exceeded their parse time budget at p99

outputs.name: total_formats_tested
outputs.description: Total number of formats tested

assertions.message: No format may exceed its parse time budget at p99
All 17 format parsers must be tested
All format parse time results must be valid
